seoTitle: Island Lagoon vs Jabuk — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Island Lagoon and Jabuk. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Island Lagoon vs Jabuk: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Island Lagoon and Jabuk.
heading: Island Lagoon vs Jabuk — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Island Lagoon, South Australia and Jabuk, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jabuk has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Island Lagoon (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Island Lagoon or Jabuk safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Island Lagoon has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Jabuk scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Island Lagoon scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Jabuk scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Island Lagoon or Jabuk?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jabuk has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
